transcript: the ability to say no
have you ever struggled when you tried to say no to someone
or perhaps you couldnt say no to a person because you felt bad for them
it seems like its not a big deal but sometimes it can cause really serious problems
learning to say no is one of the skills which empowers us the most we that can improve it allows us to take control of our lives set boundaries and prioritize what truly matters
lets begin by addressing the question why is it so hard to say no
from a young age most of us are taught to be agreeable to avoid conflict and to please others saying yes often feels like the easier safer option
we worry about disappointing people damaging relationships or being perceived as selfish
this fear of rejection or judgment often leads us to overcommit even when it comes at the expense of our own well being
saying yes makes us feel useful wanted and accepted but in trying to be everything to everyone we risk losing ourselves

now lets talk about what happens when we say yes too often on the surface agreeing to every request might seem like the polite or productive thing to do however over time it can lead to burnout stress and resentment
imagine this youre juggling work deadlines family responsibilities and personal goals a friend asks you to help them with a project someone wants you to take part of their presentation
your friend invites you to an event you dont have the energy to attend instead of saying no you say yes to all of them the result
you spread yourself too thin you feel exhausted overwhelmed and frustrated not just with others but with yourself
when we say yes to things that dont align with our priorities were effectively saying no to the things that do we sacrifice our time energy and even our mental health
think about the most successful people you admire they didnt get there by agreeing to everything
they got there by focusing their time and energy on what mattered most to them they understood that saying no to distractions and unnecessary obligations was the key to achieving their dreams
of course knowing the importance of saying no is one thing actually doing it is another
so how can we say no effectively without feeling guilty or causing unnecessary conflict
first of all we should be clear direct and avoid long explanations or excuses a simple polite response like im sorry i cant do this right now is often enough

you can also offer an alternative if its appropriate if you genuinely want to help but cant fulfill the request suggest another solution for example i cant do it but maybe someone else on the team can help

and never forget to stand firm people may try to convince you to change your mind but remember you have the right to prioritize yourself saying no doesnt make you a bad person it makes you a responsible one
when you start saying no more often youll notice some incredible changes in your life
youll have more time and energy to dedicate to the things that truly matter to you people will value your time and honesty
youll feel less overwhelmed and more in control of your life
one of the biggest obstacles to saying no is guilt we worry about letting others down or being perceived as unkind
but saying no doesnt mean you dont care about others it means you care enough about yourself to prioritize your needs
think about it this way you cant pour from an empty cup
if you constantly say yes to everyone else youll eventually run out of time and energy for yourself and when youre depleted youre no longer good to anyone

so the next time youre faced with a request that you dont want to accept remember this saying no isnt selfish its necessary its a powerful act of self care that allows you to say yes to what truly matters
lets start viewing no not as a negative word but as a positive step toward a more meaningful life
because every time you say no to something that doesnt align with your goals youre saying yes to your own happiness health and growth
thanks
